channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
Boiler has been left next to bins

item.title: When’s the grass getting cut?, 9th June
item.description:
Grass of St Mary’s Churchyard hasn’t been cut in 2 months. Please cut the grass this is what we pay council tax for. The grass is already a meter high.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Churchyard Row, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blue graffiti, 9th June
item.description:
Blue graffiti on grey shutters of London Fog

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72, Camberwell Church Street, Camberwell, Greater London

item.title: Large quantity of Lime bikes parked causing a hazard, 9th June
item.description:
There are a large quantity of Lime and Voi Bikes parked on the junction of Finsbury Market and Earl street. The bikes are spilling over onto the pavement and into the road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Appold Street, Hackney, London

item.title: 2x bollards knocked down AGAIN, 9th June
item.description:
These seem to get hit a lot. Unsure who done it but both laying down
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Heron Court, Widmore, Greater London

item.title: Nw1 9ht outside car park space 28, 9th June
item.description:
There is an tree being grown which is bending towards car parking space 28
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Agar Grove, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Entire pavement on Ravensmead Road needs relaying, 9th June
item.description:
The footways along the entire length of Ravensmead Road, on both sides of the carriageway, are in a severely deteriorated condition and require full reconstruction rather than further patch repairs. There are numerous uneven, broken and sunken paving slabs creating significant trip hazards for pedestrians. The condition of the pavement presents a safety risk to residents, visitors, elderly pedestrians, parents with pushchairs and those with mobility impairments. Residents have already experienced trips and falls, with some sustaining injuries. In addition, there are ongoing drainage issues along sections of the road. Poor surface levels and inadequate drainage result in standing water during periods of rainfall, with water pooling on footways and, in some locations, running towards adjacent residential properties. The current condition of the footways has reached the point where repeated patching is no longer an effective solution. A full assessment of Ravensmead Road is requested, with consideration given to comprehensive footway reconstruction, re-levelling and improved drainage to provide a safe, accessible and durable walking surface for all users.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensmead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 9th June
item.description:
Dog fouling near wall of property and around tree near 16 Maple Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Maple Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tress overhanging the pavement to below head height., 9th June
item.description:
Trees are overhanging the pavement and road to below head height for a distance of at least 20m. This means that pedestrians have to walk into the road to avoid them which is affecting the elderly and people with push chairs quite badly.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace Grove, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A large black bag full of rubbish has been dumped as well as some cat litter, 9th June
item.description:
A large black bag full of rubbish was dumped at the weekend 8 June. This is now attracting more rubbish e,g, a cushion. The black bag was dumped on top of some car litter.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King William Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement in front of 73 Dyne Road, NW6 7DR, 9th June
item.description:
Dead fox
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dyne Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Dead animal on street

item.title: Fly tipping from residents and local builders working in maylands drive sidcup, 9th June
item.description:
Mainly at Weekends, School holidays and Bank Holidays I feel someone has read this as it's gone quiet in Maylands Drive it will not stop till the culprits are caught. Kids on electric scooters bikes and cars speeding building not legally registered dumping their waste down the alley connecting Maylands Drive and Cleve Road.Cars not insured unregistered businesses Maylands Drive Sidcup is awfull for noise and pollution Fly tipping alleyway between cleve road da14 4rr and maylands drive da14 4rn Fly tipping alleyway between cleve road da14 4rr and maylands drive da14 4rn Builders constantly working in Maylands Drive during the week, weekends and Bank Holidays all day then unauthorised dumping of material, Cans bins, settees chairs in the alley connecting Maylands Drive DA14 4RN and Cleve Road DA14 4RR.Been going on for at least 5 yrs rubbish dumped very late at night or early hours of the morning.Alley needs notices monitoring and cameras. Builders noise effecting residents at Harefield Road DA14 4RJ and Goodwin Drive DA14 4NX.Loud speeding unlicesenced cars , electric scooters by kids motor bikes all no insurance even QUAD BIKES effecting St Andrews DA14 4SA and DA14 4RX, Cleve Road DA14 4RR and Kimberley Drive DA14 4PP and DA14 4QF residents totally annoyed happens all hours. The report's reference number is 9571562. Please quote this if you need to contact the council about this report. This email was sent automatically, from an unmonitored email account. Please do not reply to it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 125, Maylands Drive, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Hedge O/S 65 Granville Road Welling, 9th June
item.description:
Overgrown Hedge O/S 65 Granville Road Resident advised overgrown hedge which is causing people to walk onto the road to pass She also advise there is a broken pavement slab, at top of same road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Granville Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags left by the council's own team for days, 9th June
item.description:
This flytip is by the council's own street cleaning team. Part of it has been there over a week and more added 4 days ago. Could you please not do this as this is a flytipping hot-spot anyway so you are only encouraging it!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Parkdale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Left for weeks unused, 9th June
item.description:
This has no valid tax

item.title: Black plastic bags of rubble, 9th June
item.description:
Black plastic bags of rubble dumped against tree in Melvin Road but near back gate of empty Croydon Road property

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Melvin Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked Drain Causing Flooding in Car Park (Blocks 24–31), 9th June
item.description:
The drain located in the Shere House car park, near blocks 24–31, is blocked and is barely visible due to the buildup around it. During rainfall, water cannot drain properly, causing a large area of the car park to become flooded and unusable. Please arrange for the drain to be cleared and the area inspected as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Great Dover Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
